title: senecio sipoccruncus
intro: Senecio sipoccruncus, also known as Groundsel or Butterweed, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to various regions around the world, including North America and Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes. In herbal preparations, Groundsel has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications.
